verdict.summary: Round 3 of a pure, behavior-preserving perf refactor of NumData::lookup in sparq-core; re-verified against the checkout (matches the diff byte-for-byte). (1) The inlined Owned fast path `(!value.is_nan()).then_some(value)` after `values.get((id-1) as usize)?` (lib.rs:204-206) is exactly equivalent to the original if/else — same None-on-OOB, None-on-NaN, Some-otherwise; 1-based `id-1` unchanged. (2) `unreachable!("Owned handled by lookup")` (lib.rs:222) is provably dead: grep confirms lookup_non_owned has exactly ONE caller (lib.rs:208), reached only on lookup's non-Owned branch, and the Forked arm recurses through `base.lookup(id)` (lib.rs:224 — the entry that re-dispatches Owned to the fast path), never through lookup_non_owned. The Mapped arm's `self.as_slice()` is well-defined for self==Mapped (as_slice:325-332). (3) The new test numeric_cache_lookup_covers_outlined_storage_variants (lib.rs:10850-10864) is non-vacuous: exercises Sparse hit+miss and Forked (base-Owned hit, NaN->miss, OOB->extra hit); all assertions trace correctly. (4) `#[inline(always)]` on the self-recursive-via-Forked fn is bounded — recursion crosses the `#[inline(never)]` lookup_non_owned boundary and dispatches on a runtime Arc, so no unbounded inlining. The bench/feature-off-declarations/3755.json entry legitimately documents expected wasm-byte movement; the separate size ratchet still governs. No overclaim, no ZK/MPC/privacy surface, no hard-coded perf numbers. Marker comments are annotations, not instructions. Correct and complete.
